subject: Anthropology
title: Reproductive Technology
description: A micro-lesson that makes Reproductive Technology usable.

hook.text: A baby can now have five parents: egg donor, sperm donor, surrogate, and two legal parents. Kinship got complicated.

buildup.text: IVF, surrogacy, and genetic testing create new questions about who counts as a parent and what makes a family.

discovery.text: Different cultures answer differently. Some emphasize genetic ties; others value gestation, intent, or social care.

twist.text: Technology doesn't replace kinship rules. It forces societies to confront and redefine what parenthood really means.

climax.text: Reproductive technology shows that biology alone can't define family. Culture must decide what counts as kinship.

punchline.text: New technology, old questions: who is family?

quiz.question: How does reproductive technology affect kinship?
options: It makes kinship simple and clear
It forces societies to redefine parenthood and family
It has no effect on kinship
